title: Justicia stearnii
taxonomy: Justicia stearnii is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Justicia, which comprises various species of flowers. The classification of Justicia stearnii further places it in the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants. Within the Acanthaceae family, Justicia stearnii can be distinguished from other species through its unique characteristics and features. Further detailed classification may involve its position within the Acanthaceae family and its phylogenetic relationships with other species.

intro: Justicia stearnii, also known as Stearn's Justicia or Stearn's snout flower, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Justicia stearnii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
